_sample.mode_name: Mode 4: Body Type With Multiple Garments
_sample.how_mode_is_detected: Use this when mode is body_type_garments and both garments/og/<id>.jpg and garments/og/<id>/ exist.
input_files: garments/og/<id>.jpg as the original body/reference image shown first in the reel
garments/og/<id>/ containing the garments to try on
archetypes.body_type_model as the similar avatar/body model used for all VTO calls
flow: Normalize the original body/reference image to garments/og/<id>.normalized.jpg.
Try each normalized garment on the one body/avatar model image.
Build a reel with the normalized body/reference intro, generated VTO images, and optional end card.
_sample.reel_note: Mode 4 uses a special centered intro banner placed around the 3/4 vertical point of the original body/reference image.

_labels.generated_result_labels: Each generated VTO clip displays the garment filename as a safe right-side label around the 3/4 vertical point.
_labels.filename_tip: Use readable garment filenames such as ivory_halter.jpg or red_saree.jpg because those names become reel labels.

vto.prompt: virtual try on. keep the same body type, face, hair, and overall likeness from the model image. vary the pose slightly for each outfit to create natural movement, like a real editorial fitting sequence, with subtle shifts in stance, arms, shoulders, and body angle. avoid a stiff doll-like repeated pose. show each garment naturally fitted on the body. preserve realistic fabric, proportions, lighting, and full outfit detail.
